title: DHS OIG Announces 8 Active Immigration Enforcement Oversight Reviews
excerpt: The DHS Office of Inspector General publicly announced it was conducting eight concurrent oversight reviews of immigration enforcement programs, including ICE's hiring and training surge, its excessive-force investigation processes, and unannounced detention facility inspections. The OIG stated reviews were being conducted "as expeditiously as possible" but provided no completion timeline.
content:
On February 5, 2026, the DHS Office of Inspector General (OIG) publicly disclosed that it was actively conducting eight concurrent oversight reviews of DHS immigration enforcement operations. This was the most expansive simultaneous immigration oversight portfolio the OIG had publicly announced.

  • The eight reviews covered: (1) ICE's hiring and training program surge capacity; (2) ICE's 287(g) program; (3) ICE's use of expedited removal authority; (4) CBP's interior immigration enforcement; (5) ICE's processes for investigating allegations of excessive force; (6) DHS's processes for preventing the arrest of U.S. citizens; (7) unannounced inspections of ICE detention facilities; and (8) DHS's management of biometrics and personally identifiable information.
  • The announcement came amid the fatal shootings of U.S. citizens Renee Good and Alex Pretti by federal agents in Minneapolis, and growing congressional concern about ICE accountability.
  • The OIG stated it would inform DHS and Congress of any findings that "warrant immediate attention and action" before issuing final reports.
  • The OIG's office simultaneously faced significant staffing constraints, having lost approximately 100 staff members between 2024 and 2026 according to OPM data.
The OIG stated reviews were "being conducted as expeditiously as possible while ensuring we apply rigor and uphold our professional standards."
source: DHS Office of Inspector General / Federal News Network
mediaPlaceholder: OIG press release listing the eight active immigration enforcement oversight reviews
